payload.moduleName: Dynamic Lighting System with Directional Light Shadows
payload.description: Implement a performant 2D lighting engine that casts real-time shadows from the player and major entities using offscreen canvas compositing with 'multiply' blend mode. The system maintains a 512×512 shadow map updated every 4 frames, rendering light rays from player position (g._cam.x, g._cam.y) with 8-16 dynamic light sources (enemy spawns, absorption field edges, score milestones). Use radial gradients on offscreen canvas with alpha falloff (0.3–0.8 intensity) composited back via ctx.globalCompositeOperation='multiply', darkening background grid and creating volumetric depth. Integrate with g._ecs to track light-emitting entities and g._palette for color-correct shadow tints. Visual impact: enemies cast subtle dark halos on grid, absorption field glows with golden radiance, scored pixels emit brief light bursts. This elevates visual hierarchy, adds atmospheric cinematic quality, and makes the 1-bit aesthetic feel dimensionally rich without breaking performance (<2ms per frame on 60fps target).

payload.description: Post-processing overlay that runs last. Scanlines: draw semi-transparent dark horizontal lines every 3px (opacity 0.03). Vignette: radial gradient from transparent center to dark edges. All effects subtle — enhance not distract. Toggle with g._fx.crt.

payload.description: Implement a post-processing shader effect that warps and fragments enemy movement trails based on player absorption proximity, creating visual feedback for energy drain zones. Use offscreen canvas at 0.5x resolution to render enemy trails with `globalCompositeOperation='lighter'` for additive blending, then apply a radial gradient mask centered on player position (g._cam.x, g._cam.y) that progressively distorts trail vertices using sine-wave displacement (amplitude: 2-8px, frequency tied to absorption field radius). Integrate with g._ecs to query all enemy entities within 200px and modulate trail opacity from 100% → 30% as enemies approach absorption field threshold, creating visual 'pull' sensation. Composite final trail layer back to main canvas using `'screen'` blend mode with 0.8 alpha. This adds 3-4ms per frame while dramatically improving game feel—enemies appear caught in magnetic field rather than freely moving.

payload.moduleName: Dynamic Focal Depth with Depth-of-Field Blurring
payload.description: Implement a depth-of-field post-processing effect that renders entities into layered depth planes (foreground, mid, background) based on their world Y-position or explicit depth component. Use offscreen canvas with variable blur radius (0-8px via Gaussian kernel) applied selectively to background/mid layers while keeping player and priority targets sharp. Integrate with g._cam to track focal depth around player position, updating dynamically as camera moves. Composite final frame using globalAlpha blending and source-over mode to layer focused/blurred planes. Adds atmospheric cinematic feel (+40% visual polish), improves visual hierarchy clarity for crowded scenes, and creates perceivable depth separation without performance cost (single 2-pass offscreen render per frame). Configure depth bands as tunable constants: sharp zone ±120px around focal point, progressive blur falloff beyond.

payload.moduleName: Dynamic Directional Audio Spatial Cues System
payload.description: Implement a procedurally-generated spatial audio layer that uses canvas-rendered directional indicators and frequency-mapped visual feedback to guide player attention toward threats/opportunities. Create an offscreen canvas (256x256) that samples enemy positions relative to g._cam and g._ecs, rendering animated arrow glyphs at screen edges (8px inset) that pulse in intensity based on threat proximity (0-255 alpha mapped to distance 0-2000px) and frequency-shift color (red=aggressive enemies, cyan=collectibles, yellow=hazards). Layer atop existing HUD using g._renderLayers with composite 'screen' mode. Integrate with g._particleEngine to emit 3-4 directional sparkles per cue update (60Hz) that streak toward indicated direction. Synchronize visual pulse frequency (0.3-2.0Hz) with adaptive difficulty multiplier to reinforce tension escalation. This bridges gap between screen-based gameplay and meta-awareness, reducing frustration and increasing flow state—critical for alpha retention.

payload.moduleName: Temporal Slowdown Field with Chromatic Decay
payload.description: Implement a screen-wide time dilation mechanic triggered by high-combo absorption events (15+ chain) that selectively slows non-player entities to 0.3x speed for 800ms while player maintains full speed, creating risk/reward gameplay depth. Render via offscreen canvas with per-entity speed multipliers stored in ECS, apply post-processing chromatic aberration that intensifies with slowdown strength (shift RGB channels by 2-4px based on time remaining), and layer a subtle purple radial gradient vignette (rgba(100,50,200,0.15)) emanating from player position using ctx.createRadialGradient(). On slowdown end, trigger rapid particle burst (200 particles, velocity 3-8px/frame in all directions) with additive blending to punctuate the temporal release. Integrate with Combo Chain System to auto-trigger at thresholds, update g._deltaTime multiplier for affected entities, and emit audio spatial cue (deep bass drop at 60Hz frequency). This creates visceral 'bullet-time' juice while maintaining performance via spatial hash pre-filtering of affected entities.

payload.moduleName: Pixel Pulse Health Bar with Damage Flash
payload.description: A compact health indicator displayed as a horizontal pixel bar beneath the player's position, filled with glowing squares that deplete on damage and gradually regenerate. When the player takes damage, the bar flashes red and triggers a brief localized screen shake. The bar uses a distinct color (cyan or magenta) that contrasts with the grid background, with each pixel segment representing 10% health. This provides clear visual feedback of the player's survival state without cluttering the HUD, and synergizes with existing Screen Shake VFX and Adaptive Difficulty systems.
